intro:
Unfortunately, I was unable to find any information on the plant Pyrolirion arvense. However, I found Pyrola, the closest match to this name. 

Here is a paragraph about Pyrola:

Pyrola, also known as Pyrola species, is a type of flowering plant in the heath family (Ericaceae). The roots and leaves of Pyrola have been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including digestive problems and respiratory infections, due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Pyrola is rich in bioactive constituents such as phenolic acids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Pyrola include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health issues.
